data.title: Sherwin-Williams (SHW) Net Margin Compression Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narratives
data.description:
Sherwin-Williams (SHW) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$5.7b and basic EPS of US$2.18, alongside net income of US$534.7m, setting the tone for how investors will read the rest of the year’s earnings story. The company’s quarterly revenue moved from US$5.3b in Q1 2025 and US$5.3b in Q4 2024 to US$5.7b in Q1 2026, while basic EPS shifted over the same periods from US$2.02 to US$1.92 and now US$2.18. This puts the focus squarely on how consistently Sherwin-Williams converts this top line into margins.

See our full analysis for Sherwin-Williams.

With the latest results on the table, the next step is to see how these margins and growth trends line up with the most widely shared narratives around Sherwin-Williams and where those stories might need updating.

Margins Set The Tone At 10.9% Net Level

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Sherwin-Williams generated US$23.9b of revenue and US$2.6b of net income, which works out to a 10.9% net profit margin compared with 11.6% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative points to cost controls and restructuring as key to better profitability, and the current 10.9% margin gives a clear benchmark:
    • Analysts expect revenue to grow about 4.4% per year and earnings about 9% per year, so any future margin move away from 10.9% will quickly show whether those expectations are on track.
    • Against five year earnings growth of roughly 7.3% a year and the recent period of negative earnings, the current margin level is a concrete reference point for how quickly any efficiency gains or volume recovery are flowing through.

EPS Trend Versus Bullish Growth Story

  • Basic EPS in Q1 2026 came in at US$2.18, compared with US$1.94 in Q4 2025 and US$2.02 in Q1 2025, while trailing 12 month EPS sits at US$10.54.
  • Bulls argue that investments in efficiency, automation and premium coatings can support long term earnings growth, and the recent EPS pattern gives some context for that view:
    • Quarterly EPS has moved between US$1.94 and US$3.38 over the last year, so the Q1 2026 figure is within that range rather than a clear breakout that would strongly confirm a sharp acceleration in earnings.
    • With trailing net income of US$2.6b already in place, the bullish idea of much higher future earnings implies a meaningful step up from what is currently being earned, which investors can now compare directly to this latest EPS run rate.
On top of these EPS trends, bulls and optimists often look at how store expansion, new products and margin initiatives might change the story over several years, not just one quarter, and that broader argument is laid out in the 🐂 Sherwin-Williams Bull Case

Debt Load And Bearish Concerns On Profit Quality

  • The latest risk summary flags a high level of debt on the balance sheet alongside a move in trailing net profit margin from 11.6% to 10.9% over the last year.
  • Bears focus on pressure in some segments and higher costs as potential threats to earnings quality, and the debt flag plus margin slip feed into that:
    • Concerns about rising tariffs and flat raw material costs potentially lifting input costs are easier to weigh when margins are already 0.7 percentage points lower than the prior 11.6% level.
    • The combination of elevated leverage and modest margin compression means the cautious view has concrete reference points, even though Q1 2026 net income of US$534.7m still fits within the recent quarterly range.

data.title: Paccar (PCAR) Margin Compression To 8.9% Tests Bullish Growth Narrative
data.description:
PACCAR (PCAR) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$6.8b and basic EPS of US$1.15, while trailing twelve month revenue stood at US$27.8b and basic EPS at US$4.71, setting a clear earnings benchmark for investors watching the heavy truck cycle. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue fluctuate between US$6.7b and US$7.9b, with quarterly basic EPS ranging from US$0.96 to US$1.66. This has occurred alongside five year EPS growth of about 11.7% per year and earnings forecasts around 15.9% annually. Together, these figures put the Q1 print into a broader growth context, even as net profit margins have moved lower and keep the focus firmly on profitability quality.

See our full analysis for PACCAR.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how they line up against the widely held narratives about PACCAR's growth potential, risks, and earnings resilience.

See what the community is saying about PACCAR

Advertisement

Margins Ease Back From 10.7% To 8.9%

  • On a trailing twelve month view, PACCAR converted US$27.8b of revenue into US$2.5b of net income, which works out to an 8.9% net profit margin compared with 10.7% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative expects expanding parts and services to support margin strength, yet the step down in margin from 10.7% to 8.9% sits against that view and raises two questions:
    • Record parts performance and recurring revenue are cited as supports for earnings stability, but the margin line in the trailing numbers reflects cost pressure that is not captured in those high level growth drivers.
    • Analysts also expect profit margins to reach 12.4% in a few years, so the current 8.9% starting point means a sizeable gap that relies on that higher margin business mix actually flowing through.

EPS Trends Versus Bullish Growth Story

  • Q1 2026 basic EPS of US$1.15 sits within a recent range of US$0.96 to US$1.66 per quarter, while five year EPS growth averaged about 11.7% per year and earnings are forecast at roughly 15.9% annual growth off a trailing EPS base of US$4.71.
  • Bullish investors argue that tighter emissions rules and higher value trucks can push earnings to US$5.4b by around 2028, and the current numbers partly test that view:
    • The latest trailing EPS of US$4.71 and net income of US$2.5b provide a concrete starting point, so lifting to earnings above US$5b would require a step up in profit that is larger than the growth seen in the last five year trend.
    • At the same time, the 11.7% historical EPS growth and roughly 15.9% forecast earnings growth are consistent with a growth story, so bulls can point to that track record even though the quarterly EPS range shows that progress has not been a straight line.
Have a closer look at how optimistic investors connect these growth targets back to PACCAR's current EPS and margin profile before forming your own view 🐂 PACCAR Bull Case

Debt, Dividend Flows And The Bear Case

  • Risk flags in the data call out a high level of debt and an unstable dividend record, alongside net profit margins that moved from 10.7% to 8.9% over the past year.
  • Bears worry that technology change and tougher regulation will pressure returns, and the current risk profile interacts with that concern in a few ways:
    • Critics highlight that softer margins combined with high leverage can make earnings more sensitive to any period of weaker revenue growth than the 6.7% annual revenue forecast suggests.
    • The description of the dividend as unstable means income focused holders cannot assume a smooth payout path if cash flows come under pressure, which is consistent with a cautious view on long term payout reliability.

data.title: Axogen (AXGN) Q1 Loss Of US$0.38 EPS Tests Bullish Margin Improvement Narrative
data.description:
Axogen (AXGN) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$61.5 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.38, alongside a net loss of US$19.6 million. This keeps the focus firmly on how fast the top line can support a path back toward profitability. Over the past five reported quarters, revenue has moved from US$48.6 million in Q1 2025 to US$61.5 million in Q1 2026, while basic EPS has swung between a small profit of US$0.02 and losses, including US$0.08 in Q1 2025 and US$0.38 this quarter. In that context, the latest numbers put the spotlight on whether Axogen can pull more operating leverage from its sales base and start closing the gap in its margins.

See our full analysis for Axogen.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to line these results up against the most common Axogen narratives to see which stories the numbers support and which ones start to look overstretched.

See what the community is saying about Axogen

Advertisement

Losses widen again on trailing numbers

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Axogen reported total revenue of US$238.1 million and a net loss of US$31.5 million, compared with US$225.2 million of revenue and a US$15.7 million loss for the prior trailing period.
  • Consensus narrative expects expanding market adoption and better margins over time, yet the latest trailing figures still show earnings in the red, which creates a tension between:
    • Forecasts that earnings could grow strongly with a path to profitability within three years, while the most recent trailing EPS is a loss of US$0.66 per share.
    • The idea of improving operational leverage, set against net losses that have widened on the latest 12 month view compared with the prior trailing period.

Quarterly swing from small profit to larger loss

  • Q1 2026 net loss was US$19.6 million on revenue of US$61.5 million, compared with Q3 2025 net income of US$0.7 million on US$60.1 million of revenue and Q4 2025 net loss of US$13.2 million on US$59.9 million of revenue.
  • Bullish views highlight potential for better operating leverage as sales build, yet the recent pattern of quarterly results raises questions about how smooth that path might be, because:
    • Profitability flipped from a small profit in Q2 and Q3 2025, with EPS of about US$0.01 per share in each of those quarters, to losses again in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, including a Q1 2026 loss of US$0.38 per share.
    • The move from roughly breakeven to a larger quarterly loss suggests the margin profile is still sensitive even at revenue levels around US$60 million per quarter.
Curious how this earnings swing fits into the bullish case that highlights margin improvement and long term growth potential, and see how supporters frame the upside in spite of the latest loss print: 🐂 Axogen Bull Case.

  • Bearish arguments focus on valuation and dilution, and the current data provide clear support for that caution, because:

    • The gap between the current price and the DCF fair value, alongside higher P/S multiples than peers and the broader industry, underpins concerns that a lot of the projected growth is already reflected in the valuation.

data.title: METAWATER Co., Ltd. (TSE:9551) Analysts Are Pretty Bullish On The Stock After Recent Results
data.description:
Last week, you might have seen that METAWATER Co., Ltd. (TSE:9551) released its full-year result to the market. The early response was not positive, with shares down 7.5% to JP¥3,415 in the past week. Results overall were respectable, with statutory earnings of JP¥209 per share roughly in line with what the analysts had forecast. Revenues of JP¥210b came in 3.4% ahead of analyst predictions. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. So we gathered the latest post-earnings forecasts to see what estimates suggest is in store for next year.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Following the latest results, METAWATER's five analysts are now forecasting revenues of JP¥220.7b in 2027. This would be a modest 5.2% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to increase 5.0% to JP¥220. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of JP¥217.4b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥209 in 2027. So the consensus seems to have become somewhat more optimistic on METAWATER's earnings potential following these results.

Check out our latest analysis for METAWATER

The consensus price target rose 17% to JP¥3,100, suggesting that higher earnings estimates flow through to the stock's valuation as well. It could also be instructive to look at the range of analyst estimates, to evaluate how different the outlier opinions are from the mean. Currently, the most bullish analyst values METAWATER at JP¥3,800 per share, while the most bearish prices it at JP¥2,000. Analysts definitely have varying views on the business, but the spread of estimates is not wide enough in our view to suggest that extreme outcomes could await METAWATER shareholders.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. We would highlight that METAWATER's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 5.2% annualised growth rate until the end of 2027 being well below the historical 9.0% p.a. growth over the last five years. Compare this to the 247 other companies in this industry with analyst coverage, which are forecast to grow their revenue at 5.7% per year. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it looks like METAWATER is forecast to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The biggest takeaway for us is the consensus earnings per share upgrade, which suggests a clear improvement in sentiment around METAWATER's earnings potential next year. They also reconfirmed their revenue estimates, with the company predicted to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

With that said, the long-term trajectory of the company's earnings is a lot more important than next year. At Simply Wall St, we have a full range of analyst estimates for METAWATER going out to 2029, and you can see them free on our platform here..

We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 1 warning sign for METAWATER that you need to be mindful of.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider AL Sydbank (CPSE:ALSYDB) After Its 37% One-Year Surge?
data.description:

  • The share price has moved around recently, with a 3% decline over the last 7 days, an 8.4% gain over the last 30 days, a 4.6% decline year to date, and a 37.2% return over the past year, capped by very large gains over 3 and 5 years.

For AL Sydbank, this context matters because it can influence how the market treats traditional valuation metrics such as P/E, P/B, and dividend yield.

Approach 1: AL Sydbank Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much value a bank can create over and above the return that shareholders require. It starts with the equity invested in the business and compares the returns generated on that equity to the estimated cost of equity.

For AL Sydbank, the model uses a Book Value of DKK416.84 per share and an Average Return on Equity of 12.81%. This leads to a Stable EPS estimate of DKK59.88 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 4 analysts. The Cost of Equity is set at DKK28.83 per share, which implies an Excess Return of DKK31.05 per share. The Stable Book Value used in the model is DKK467.39 per share, also drawn from weighted future Book Value estimates from 4 analysts.

Combining these inputs, the Excess Returns valuation points to an intrinsic value of roughly DKK1,187.97 per share. With a current share price of around DKK544.50, this corresponds to the stock being 54.2% undervalued according to this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: AL Sydbank Price vs Earnings

For a profitable bank like AL Sydbank, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is already generating. In general, higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth expectations or higher risk usually align with a lower, more conservative P/E.

AL Sydbank currently trades on a P/E of 25.37x. This is above the Danish Banks industry average P/E of 11.01x and also above the peer average of 10.10x. Comparing the actual P/E of 25.37x with the Fair Ratio of 17.74x suggests the shares are trading above what the company’s characteristics would typically support.

data.title: Mondelez Shareholder Votes Test Board Independence And Plastics Oversight Priorities
data.description:
• Mondelez International (NasdaqGS:MDLZ) shareholders are set to vote at the 2026 annual meeting on a proposal calling for an independent board chairman.
  • A separate proposal asks for an objective evaluation of the company’s plastics packaging practices and related policies.
  • The board has recommended that shareholders vote against both proposals, signaling its stance on current governance and sustainability frameworks.

Mondelez International, the snacks and confectionery group behind brands such as Oreo and Cadbury, sits at the center of long running discussions on corporate governance and environmental practices. Consumer goods companies have been under pressure to address packaging waste and board independence, and these proposals bring those themes directly to the shareholder ballot for NasdaqGS:MDLZ.

For investors, the upcoming votes serve as a real time marker of how much influence governance and plastics policy concerns have within the Mondelez shareholder base. The outcomes may shape future engagement between the board and investors on oversight, risk management, and ESG transparency, which many shareholders track alongside financial results when assessing a company.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Mondelez International by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Mondelez International.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Mondelez International following this latest news.

These shareholder proposals arrive just as Mondelez is reporting higher Q1 2026 sales of US$10.08b and net income of US$560m, so the debate is less about short term performance and more about how the company is run and how it approaches long lived packaging issues. For you as an investor, the votes give a real time check on how much priority fellow shareholders place on an independent chair and on additional plastics reporting versus keeping the current governance and disclosure set up. The board’s decision to oppose both items, while highlighting its Lead Independent Director structure and existing packaging disclosures, indicates confidence in the current model and concern about extra cost or complexity. The size of the vote for or against each proposal can indicate how engagement might evolve, including whether larger institutions want stronger oversight tools around ESG topics alongside financial metrics such as earnings per share.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Mondelez International Narrative

  • The plastics evaluation proposal connects directly to the existing focus on sustainability initiatives as part of the company story, reinforcing that packaging and waste remain central issues for long term brand strength.
  • The push for an independent chair questions whether the current leadership structure is the best way to oversee execution on pricing, cocoa costs, and emerging market expansion that are key elements of the narrative.
  • The cost and scope of any additional plastics reporting, if eventually adopted, may not be fully captured in the existing storyline that concentrates on pricing, cocoa programs, and distribution growth.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Mondelez International to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ If a meaningful bloc of shareholders backs the independent chair proposal, it could indicate dissatisfaction with current oversight and raise questions about future governance changes.
  • ⚠️ A strong vote for more plastics reporting may point to higher expectations on packaging commitments, with potential for added reporting costs or pressure to adjust product formats over time.
  • 🎁 Clear rejection of the proposals could be interpreted as support for the existing board structure and governance practices, reducing near term uncertainty around leadership changes.
  • 🎁 Constructive engagement on plastics, regardless of the vote outcome, may help protect brand equity for Oreo, Cadbury, and other key lines as consumers focus more on packaging waste.

What To Watch Going Forward

After the 2026 annual meeting, pay close attention to the final vote tallies on both proposals and any board response, especially if support rises compared with earlier similar resolutions. Watch for comments from large institutional holders on how they interpreted the outcome, and whether Mondelez updates its governance disclosures or plastics packaging reporting in future filings. It is also useful to track how these debates line up with the company’s earnings updates, packaging initiatives, and commentary on ESG priorities during results calls.

data.title: Brown And Brown (BRO) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative In Q1 2026
data.description:
Brown & Brown (BRO) kicked off Q1 2026 with total revenue of US$1.9 billion and basic EPS of US$1.27, alongside trailing twelve month figures of US$6.3 billion in revenue and US$3.53 in EPS that frame the latest quarter in a bigger context. Over recent periods, revenue has moved from US$1.2 billion in Q4 2024 to US$1.6 billion in Q4 2025 and US$1.9 billion in Q1 2026. Quarterly basic EPS has shifted from US$0.73 to US$0.78 and now US$1.27, setting up a story where investors will be weighing earnings power against how margins are holding up.

See our full analysis for Brown & Brown.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed growth, risk, and valuation narratives around Brown & Brown.

See what the community is saying about Brown & Brown

Advertisement

Net income and margins cooling from prior year

  • On a trailing basis, Brown & Brown earned US$1.1b of net income on US$6.3b of revenue, which works out to an 18.2% net profit margin compared with 21% in the prior year.
  • Analysts' consensus view points to earnings stability supported by cost discipline and debt repayment, yet the 18.2% trailing margin versus 21% a year ago and quarterly net income of US$421 million versus US$327 million in Q1 2025 shows that margin compression is sitting alongside higher profit dollars.
    • Consensus commentary highlights an improved EBITDAC margin by 110 basis points and reduced interest expense, while the reported net margin has moved lower year on year, so efficiency gains are being partially offset elsewhere in the income statement.
    • The same consensus view talks about revenue opportunities in employee benefits and CAT property, but the current 18.2% net margin suggests that any benefit from those areas is not fully flowing through to the bottom line yet.

Multi year earnings growth vs slower outlook

  • Over the past five years, earnings grew at 15.9% per year, while trailing 12 month earnings growth sits at 11.6% and forecasts point to around 8.2% earnings growth and 8.3% revenue growth per year.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative leans positive on future expansion through acquisitions and strong demand in employee benefits, but the moderation from 15.9% historical earnings growth to 11.6% recently and about 8.2% expected highlights that the growth story is becoming more gradual than the past run rate.
    • The company completed 13 acquisitions with projected annual revenue of US$36 million, which is small compared with US$6.3b of trailing revenue, so these deals may fine tune growth more than transform it.
    • Consensus expects ongoing demand for benefits consulting and broader revenue expansion, yet the forecast 8.3% revenue growth remains below broader US market forecasts cited, so investors are being asked to pay attention to quality of growth rather than sheer speed.
Brown & Brown's earnings path tells only part of the story, and many investors are watching how the community weighs growth, margins, and valuation in one place, especially after this set of numbers. See what the community is saying about Brown & Brown.

P/E premium and DCF gap to current price

  • At a share price of US$63.14, the trailing P/E of 18.8x sits above the US Insurance industry average of 11.6x but below the peer group average of 35.7x, while a DCF fair value of US$138.64 and an analyst target of US$78.33 both stand above the current price.
  • Consensus narrative notes that effective cost management and cash generation help support the case for this valuation, yet the premium P/E, 18.2% net margin that is lower than last year, and balance sheet flags around debt coverage and shareholder dilution all mean the market is weighing mixed signals rather than a one way quality story.
    • On the risk side, debt is described as not well covered by operating cash flow and shareholders were diluted over the past year, so the balance sheet is an active part of the discussion, not an afterthought.
    • On the reward side, the model DCF fair value of US$138.64 and analyst target of US$78.33 both sit above US$63.14, which contrasts with the softer margin profile and slower growth forecasts and helps explain why opinions on the current P/E premium are likely divided.
